settings.title: Late-Night Hours for the Holiday Rush
settings.excerpt: Three extra evenings a week through the festive stretch so you are not squeezing it in.
settings.body:
The calendar fills up fast around the holidays, and so do our books. To make room, Shearline is staying open late through the season.

Every year the last fortnight before the holidays turns into a scramble, with everyone wanting to look their best for the same handful of evenings. The late nights are our way of spreading that out.

When we are open late

We will be here until eight on Wednesdays, Thursdays, and Fridays, with the full team on hand. Evenings always go first, so it is worth booking ahead.

A few tips to get the slot you want:

  • Book the moment you know the date you are getting ready for.
  • Midweek evenings tend to free up before Friday does.
  • Color takes longer than a cut, so allow extra time.

Working back from your event

If you want a fresh cut or color before a particular event, tell us the date and we will work backward to find the right time for you. Color in particular is best done a day or two before rather than the same afternoon, so it has settled by the time you walk out the door.

The late nights run through the festive stretch and then we go back to normal hours in the new year. So if the evenings suit you, make the most of them while they last.
